commands.review_summary:
The PR wraps the Tailscale binary probe's existing `Promise.race` in `try/finally` and clears the manual timeout handle after the race settles.

PR surface: Source +5. Total +5 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main creates a manual `setTimeout` inside `checkBinary` and does not clear it when `runExec` wins the race. I did not execute a live Tailscale binary-discovery run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human-only until the external contributor supplies real behavior proof; there is no narrow code defect for ClawSweeper to repair automatically.

target.title: fix: clear timeout timer in Tailscale binary probe Promise.race
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where the Tailscale binary locator (`findTailscaleBinary`) would leave a dangling timer in the event loop after each successful binary check. The `checkBinary` helper races a `setTimeout`-based timeout against `runExec`, but the timeout timer was never cleared when `runExec` completed first.

## Why This Change Was Made

Wrap the `Promise.race` in a `try/finally` block that captures the timer handle and clears it on completion. This matches the pattern used elsewhere in the codebase (`src/node-host/with-timeout.ts`, `src/infra/provider-usage.shared.ts`).

## User Impact

No user-visible change. Prevents a minor resource leak during Tailscale binary discovery.

## Evidence

- **`src/infra/tailscale.test.ts`: 30/30 passed** ✅
- **`src/infra/tailnet.test.ts`: all tests pass**
- Same cleanup pattern already proven in `src/node-host/with-timeout.ts:37-45`
